raw_input: Write a weekly engineering status update (100–150 words) for a backend team. Organize the response into THREE labeled sections in this order: 'Progress:', 'Risks:', 'Next:'. Each section must be at least two sentences. Context for this week: the team shipped the migration to the new NATS queue, began the Go rewrite of the billing service, and is seeing an intermittent flake in the integration suite that blocks ~1 in 8 CI runs. Audience: the engineering director. Tone: concise, factual, no filler.
success_criteria: three labeled sections: 'Progress:', 'Risks:', 'Next:' in that order
each section at least two sentences
mentions NATS migration, Go billing rewrite, and CI flake
constraints: no filler openings like 'Here is the status update…'
